systems: an array of 24 phoswich detectors -
Ca(F-2)(Eu) followed by 38.1 cm of plastic or 1 mm
Ca(F-2) in front of 26 cm plastic scintillator, a
NaLi/Si-microstrip systems, and a NaI wire chamber
The photodisintegration of deuterium with linearly
polarized light has been measured in the energy range
of 110 - 315 MeV. The results of five independent
measurements using three detector systems, two liquid
target geometrics, and two photon energy distribution
end-points are presented. These data are combined
into one average data set with a systematic
uncertainty of +- 5 %. These statistical precision of
this combined data set, coupled with the sensitivity
to the strength of the short range tensor interaction
observed in the parallel-perpendicular cross section
difference, makes this an attractive reaction for
investigating aspects of the nn interaction. To do
this reliably requires a complete, systematic
description of the reaction through the delta region.
include fully retarded pion potentials are compared
with multipole fits to the data. These calculations
cannot reproduce the energy and angle dependence of
the data. The character of the disagreement that the
remaining problems may lie with the treatment of the
delta. Because of experimental limitations, it has
been customary to present the polarization-
independent cross section (DA) data and beam
Data were compiled at the Russia MSU SINP
